attacking_style: Descending specialist who attacks on technical downhills after summit finishes, capable of gaining 30-60 seconds through superior bike handling. Also probes on final ramps of climbs but rarely sustains long-distance solo efforts on ascents.
team_tactics: UAE employs conservative GC defense positioning, keeping him in lead groups rather than offensive moves. Occasionally used to soften groups for team leaders (attacked before Evenepoel move). Limited domestique support until earning protected status mid-Vuelta 2023.
terrain_preference: Technical descents are his weapon (gained 1 minute on Albula, 8 seconds on Suisse descent). Performs well on sustained moderate climbs but struggles significantly on extreme gradients above 15% (Angliru exposed twice, losing podium and 4+ minutes).
vulnerability: Extreme gradient climbs systematically expose him (multiple Angliru collapses). Susceptible to sustained pressure campaigns targeting his GC position (Landa battle). Shows early weakness on first portions of long climbs before finding rhythm. Time trials cost podium positions.
key_patterns: Descending attacks post-summit are signature move, multiple 30-60 second gains documented across races
Angliru/super-steep gradients repeatedly caused significant time losses and GC position drops (4th to off-podium)
Strong finisher who recovers from early climb struggles, regaining form in latter race portions
Bonus second hunting and tactical awareness evident, contests intermediate sprints when GC tight
Time trial discipline weakness: lost podium positions in TTs at Romandie and overall classifications
form_trajectory: positive: Despite Angliru setback, showed progression through 2023 Vuelta - only rider matching Vingegaard's 8km attack initially, attacking with Almeida late-race, earning full team protection. Tour de Suisse podium via descending dominance demonstrates expanding skillset.
